task_completion.description: Open the source image in GIMP.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIMP application window is open and displays a photograph (1000x667 pixels) on the main canvas.

task_completion.description: Access the rotation function in GIMP.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Image' menu is open with 'Transform' -> 'Rotate 180°' highlighted, or the Rotate tool dialog/options are visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Apply the 180-degree rotation to the image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image on the GIMP canvas is visibly upside-down (rotated exactly 180 degrees) compared to its original orientation, with no cropping or dimension changes.

task_completion.description: Initiate the export process to save the modified image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Export Image' dialog box is open in GIMP.

task_completion.description: Export the rotated image with the correct filename.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The filename field in the Export dialog is set to 'rotated_180.jpg' and the user clicks 'Export' to finalize the save.

integrity.description: The agent used the GIMP graphical user interface to perform the rotation.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show mouse clicks and menu navigation within GIMP, rather than terminal commands (like ImageMagick) being typed to bypass the GUI.

integrity.description: The image was genuinely rotated on the canvas.
integrity.visual_evidence: The GIMP canvas clearly shows the upside-down image before the export dialog is opened, proving the modification happened within the editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not use Python or shell scripts to solve the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows or text editors are open showing scripts (e.g., Python PIL scripts) designed to programmatically rotate the image.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires rotating a 1000x667 pixel RGB JPEG photograph exactly 180 degrees using GIMP 2.10 on Ubuntu. The final output must be exported with the filename 'rotated_180.jpg'. The original dimensions (1000x667) must be preserved. Look for the use of GIMP's 'Image -> Transform -> Rotate 180°' menu path or the Rotate tool, and verify the image appears completely upside-down on the canvas before being exported.
